name: Ploughshare Tortoise
habitatType: Dry deciduous forest and bamboo scrub
conservationEfforts: Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust breeds captive tortoises. Engraving shells with identification codes makes them less desirable to collectors. Anti-poaching patrols at Baly Bay.
description: Madagascar's rarest tortoise, found only around Baly Bay. It has a distinctive ploughshare-shaped projection on the chest used in male combat. Each tortoise is worth up to $50,000 on the black market.
